history.notes:
INITIAL PSYCHIATRIC EVALUATION

Chief Complaint:
29-year-old woman presents with persistent low mood, anhedonia, and decreased energy over the past 6 months.

History of Present Illness:
Sarah describes a gradual onset of depressive symptoms following a job change. Reports sleep disturbance (early morning awakening) and poor concentration. Denies history of mania or hypomania.

Past Psychiatric History:
One previous episode in college, treated with counseling only.

Family/Social History:
- Mother with history of depression.
- Lives alone, works in marketing, supportive sister nearby.

Mental Status Exam:
- Well-groomed female, speech normal rate/volume.
- Mood: 'low', affect: congruent and restricted.
- Thought process: linear. No psychosis.
- Insight and judgment: good.

Diagnosis:
Major Depressive Disorder, recurrent, moderate.

Treatment Plan:
1. Initiate Bupropion (Wellbutrin) 150mg XL daily and Escitalopram (Lexapro) 10mg daily.
2. Discussed potential side effects (GI upset, dry mouth, insomnia).
3. Safety plan discussed.
4. Scheduled automated digital check-ins every 2 days.

history.voice_note: Actually had a great day today! Feeling very energetic.
Routine is helping. I'm sticking to my morning walks.
Feeling decent. Had a good call with my mom.
Neutral day. A bit bored, but otherwise fine.
Doing okay. Work was a bit busy but manageable.
Stable. Keeping up with my meds.
My sleep is getting worse again. I'm starting to feel that heavy weight in my chest. I've been forgetting my meds the last few days.
history.actions: Agent changed frequency to daily due to worsening trend

history.voice_note: I don't even want to be here anymore. Everything feels completely pointless. I've been thinking about how I could just end it all... I can't stop these thoughts about hurting myself.
history.actions: Agent emailed provider due to high-risk self-harm ideation

history.voice_note: Still feeling very dark, but my sister stayed the night. I spoke with the crisis team.
A tiny bit better today. Saw Dr. Doe this morning for an emergency session.
history.actions: Agent reset frequency to 2 days following clinical stabilization

history.notes:
EMERGENCY FOLLOW-UP VISIT
(Triggered by Digital Alert)

Chief Complaint:
29-year-old woman presents with acute worsening of mood and suicidal ideation.

Subjective:
Sarah reports a 'downward spiral' over the last 48 hours. Triggered by a digital check-in alert where she reported self-harm thoughts. Denies active intent or plan but describes persistent 'passive' thoughts of not wanting to be here. Reports stopping medications 3 days ago due to feeling 'hopeless'.

Objective:
Patient appeared tearful and disheveled. Psychomotor retardation noted. Mood is 'desperate', affect is flat.

Assessment:
MDD, recurrent, severe, with acute suicidal ideation.

Plan:
1. Crisis intervention.
2. Safety plan updated: Sister will reside with patient for next 72 hours.
3. Medication adherence discussed: Re-start Lexapro and Wellbutrin immediately.
4. Referral to intensive outpatient program (IOP) discussed.
5. Increased digital check-in frequency to daily for high-risk monitoring. Will reassess in 72 hours.

history.voice_note: Slowly getting back to my routine. Taking it one hour at a time.
Had a much better weekend. I actually went to the park and sat in the sun for an hour.
history.actions: Agent changed frequency to 3 days due to significant mood improvement

history.voice_note: Feeling quite positive today. Energy levels are much higher. Meds are consistent.
Feeling stable today. Just a normal, okay day.
history.actions: Agent reset frequency to 2 days (default maintenance)

history.notes:
PSYCHIATRIC INTAKE NOTE

Chief Complaint:
28-year-old man presents stating, 'I can't be around people without my heart racing.'

History of Present Illness:
Michael is a 28-year-old male reporting lifelong social anxiety that has worsened recently with a new role requiring client presentations. Reports heart palpitations, sweating, and 'mind going blank' in social situations. Avoids meetings and social gatherings. No history of depressive episodes.

Social History:
Works as a software engineer, few close friends, lives with roommates.

Mental Status Exam:
Michael is cooperative but avoids eye contact. Fidgety during the interview. Speech is hesitant. Mood is 'anxious', affect is anxious. Thought process is goal-directed.

Diagnosis:
Social Anxiety Disorder (300.23).

Treatment Plan:
1. Initiate Sertraline (Zoloft) 50mg daily.
2. Initiate Buspirone 15mg BID for physical anxiety symptoms.
3. CBT-based exposure exercises discussed.
4. Deep breathing and grounding techniques taught in session.
5. Digital check-ins scheduled to monitor social anxiety triggers and medication response.

history.voice_note: I actually went to the grocery store today and didn't feel completely overwhelmed. Breathing exercises helped.
Had a team meeting at work. Spoke up once. Felt my heart racing, but I got through it.
Feeling decent today. Just stayed home and read, so no major triggers.
A friend invited me out for coffee. I went. It was exhausting but I'm glad I did it.
The medication seems to be taking the edge off. I didn't panic when the phone rang today.
Had to make a phone call to the bank. Rehearsed it three times, but it went fine.
Felt calm today. Did my mindfulness exercises this morning and it set a good tone.
Pretty good day. Walked in the park and smiled at a neighbor without freaking out.
Managed to eat lunch in the cafeteria today instead of at my desk. Small victory.
Feeling okay. The Buspirone helps when I feel the physical tension starting.
Had a conversation with a cashier and made eye contact. Feeling proud of myself.
Smooth day. No major social interactions, which gave me time to recharge.
Practiced my grounding techniques when I felt a bit anxious. They worked quickly.
Feeling pretty stable. Sleeping better definitely helps my baseline anxiety.
Attended a small gathering. Left early, but I consider showing up a win.
Feeling a bit tense today. Avoided the breakroom at work because there were too many people.
Had to cancel plans tonight. Just felt too exhausted and anxious to deal with socializing.
My chest felt tight all morning. Used my breathing techniques but still felt on edge.
A bit overwhelmed today. Too many emails and people asking me questions. Just want to hide.
Not great. Someone bumped into me and I almost had a panic attack. Took my meds.
Felt really self-conscious during a video call today. Kept my camera off.
Anxiety is bubbling up today for no specific reason. Trying to stay grounded.
Overthought a brief interaction with my manager. Can't stop replaying it in my head.
Didn't sleep well and my social battery is at zero. Skipped my morning walk to avoid people.
Feeling jittery. The physical symptoms of anxiety are really strong today.
Thinking about my upcoming presentation is making it hard to focus on anything else.
Had to excuse myself from a conversation today because I felt a panic attack coming on.
Feeling a bit discouraged. I thought I was doing better, but today was tough.
The physical symptoms (sweating, shaking) are less today, but the 'worry' is still there.
I managed to stay in a social situation for 30 minutes longer than I planned. Progress.
Feeling restless. My mind is racing about everything I might have said wrong yesterday.
A quiet day, which is exactly what I needed. Minimal social pressure.
I feel like I'm finally starting to see the 'gap' between my thoughts and my reactions.
Social situations still feel like a performance, but I'm getting better at the role.
Energy is low today. Socializing feels like a chore I'm not ready for.
Actually looking forward to a small meetup tomorrow. That's a new feeling.
The Buspirone is definitely helping with the morning jitters. Feeling more stable.
I had a minor setback today, but I used my grounding techniques and didn't spiral.
Feeling grateful for the progress I've made, even if it feels slow sometimes.
Today was a neutral day. No big wins, but no big anxieties either.
